name: Lemlist
what_it_does: Personalized cold email and multi-channel outreach platform that enables dynamic image and video personalization, LinkedIn automation, and automated follow-up sequences to improve engagement and reply rates.
use_cases: Running personalized cold email campaigns with custom images and variables per recipient
Multi-channel outreach sequences combining email, LinkedIn visits, and manual call tasks
Automating follow-up sequences with conditional branching based on whether prospects replied or opened
Integrating CRM data (Salesforce, HubSpot) to trigger outreach sequences from pipeline changes
Managing team outreach operations with shared sending accounts and team-level reporting
not_for: Transactional email or triggered notification emails (use Postmark or SendGrid)
High-volume bulk email blasts to cold lists without personalization
Inbound marketing email to opted-in subscribers (use Mailchimp or HubSpot)
Small teams on tight budgets — pricing is higher than some alternatives for similar volume
best_when: A sales team wants differentiated outreach with personalized visuals and multi-channel touchpoints (email + LinkedIn) in a single sequence workflow.
avoid_when: You need high-volume simple cold email at lowest cost, or you don't need multi-channel LinkedIn integration.

auth.notes: API key authentication passed as a Bearer token. Single key per account with no scope granularity. API keys are generated in the Lemlist dashboard under Settings > Integrations. No key expiration or rotation controls.

pricing.notes: 14-day free trial available. Per-user pricing model — costs scale with team size. LinkedIn automation requires the Multichannel Expert plan or higher.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses return JSON with a message field but error codes are inconsistent — some endpoints use numeric codes, others use string identifiers. Validation errors occasionally return 500 instead of 400 for malformed inputs. Documentation does not enumerate possible error codes.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency support. Adding leads to a campaign multiple times creates duplicates. Agents must query existing leads before adding to avoid duplication.
known_agent_gotchas: LinkedIn automation requires a LinkedIn account connected per Lemlist user — agents cannot use LinkedIn steps without a connected account
Personalization images (lemlist images) require pre-created templates — agents cannot generate image templates via API
Lead status transitions are not always exposed via API — checking if a lead replied requires webhook setup or polling
CRM sync is unidirectional in some configurations — changes in Lemlist may not flow back to CRM depending on integration settings
Team sender rotation requires explicit configuration — new API-created campaigns default to the API key owner's account only
Webhook events are limited in scope — not all campaign events trigger webhooks
API documentation has known gaps — some endpoints are undocumented or only discoverable via community forums

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. Single unscoped API key is the primary auth mechanism — no ability to limit key permissions to specific campaigns or operations. EU data residency available, GDPR DPA available on request. No audit log API for tracking key usage. LinkedIn automation credentials are stored by Lemlist, which creates additional credential custody risk.
